diagrams.title: Transmission Media Classification
diagrams.description: Shows hierarchical classification of guided and unguided media with examples
elements: Guided Media (Twisted Pair, Coaxial, Fiber Optic)
Unguided Media (Radio Waves, Microwave, Infrared, Satellite)
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the two main categories and their types

diagrams.title: Coaxial Cable Structure
diagrams.description: Cross-section view showing copper core, insulation, braided shield, and outer jacket
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing the layered construction that provides excellent shielding

diagrams.title: Twisted Pair Cable Types
diagrams.description: Shows UTP and STP construction with twist pattern and optional shielding
elements: STP: Individual pair shielding + overall shield
diagrams.purpose: Understanding EMI protection differences between UTP and STP

diagrams.title: Fiber Optic Cable Structure
diagrams.description: Shows core, cladding, and jacket layers with light ray indication
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how light propagates through fiber optics

diagrams.title: Single vs Multi-Mode Fiber
diagrams.description: Comparison showing light paths in both fiber types
elements: Single-mode: One straight light path, small core
Multi-mode: Multiple light paths, larger core
diagrams.purpose: Understanding why single-mode is for long distances and multi-mode for short distances

diagrams.title: Wireless Transmission Types
diagrams.description: Shows frequency ranges and applications for different wireless media
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing the electromagnetic spectrum used in wireless communication
